title: procedure-trial-structure
description: JEAB Procedure category, trial-structure sub-annotator. Declares session-level trial composition for free-operant schedules with mixed trial types: peak procedure (Catania & Reynolds, 1968), reinstatement tests (Baker et al., 1991), and free-operant temporal bisection (Fetterman & Killeen, 1995). Complements procedure-stimulus @testing (which applies to discrete-trial MTS only). Uses the structured-values annotation syntax (grammar.ebnf annotation_array / annotation_object) for components.

trial_mix.description: Session-level composition of a Mix/Mult schedule into trial types with per-component counts, durations, and reinforcement. Applies to free-operant Mix/Mult combinators only; for discrete-trial MTS probe testing, use @testing (procedure-stimulus) instead. The annotation captures paper-level Methods description (role-tagged components, counts, ordering specification); runtime randomization details (seed, shuffling algorithm) are delegated to execution_profile.

type.description: Trial-mix variant. 'peak': reinforced FI trials intermixed with extended unreinforced probe trials (Catania & Reynolds, 1968; Roberts, 1981). 'reinstatement': within-session response-independent reinforcer deliveries during/after extinction (Baker et al., 1991). 'temporal_bisection': short/long anchor trials intermixed with intermediate probe durations for psychophysical scaling (Fetterman & Killeen, 1995; free-operant variant of Church & Deluty, 1977).

components.description: Array of component objects, each tagging a component of the annotated Mix/Mult expression with a role and role-specific attributes. Every object MUST include 'role' (string) and 'ref' (integer, zero-based index into the Mix/Mult components). Additional attributes vary by role (see role catalog below). The annotation validator checks that the role set and per-role attributes match the declared 'type'. Schedule-level component ordering within the Mix/Mult expression is independent of the array order here; 'ref' provides the binding.

reinforced.semantics: Reinforced (standard FI) trial component of a peak procedure.

probe.semantics: Peak: extended unreinforced trial (duration = single time_value, typically 3-4x FI). Temporal bisection: intermediate-duration probe trial (durations = array<time_value> of stimulus durations).

extinction.semantics: Background extinction component of a reinstatement procedure (target operant with reinforcement withheld).

reinstatement.semantics: Response-independent reinforcer delivery component (typically FT). 'count' = number of deliveries; 'interval' = time_value override (when omitted, inferred from the referenced component's FT parameter).

short_anchor.semantics: Short-duration anchor component (reinforced; trains 'short' response class). 'duration' = stimulus duration (e.g., 2s).

long_anchor.semantics: Long-duration anchor component (reinforced; trains 'long' response class). 'duration' = stimulus duration (e.g., 8s).

ordering_spec.description: Paper-level ordering specification. 'random': trials randomly interspersed (typical for peak and temporal_bisection). 'blocked': trial types presented in separate blocks. 'fixed_sequence': deterministic paper-specified order (typical for reinstatement). Runtime implementation details (seed, shuffling algorithm) are delegated to execution_profile, preserving AST/execution orthogonality.

trial_mix.applies_to_note: Applies only to Mix/Mult combinators (free-operant response opportunity). Applying to MTS/GoNoGo trial-based primitives triggers TRIAL_MIX_WRONG_PRIMITIVE linter warning; use @testing (procedure-stimulus) for MTS probe testing. This separation reflects the paradigm incompatibility between free-operant response streams and discrete-trial procedures (see spec/en/operant/trial-based/go-nogo.md §on free-operant vs. trial-based modifiers). Component count requirement follows required_role_sets_by_type: peak requires >=2 components, reinstatement requires >=2 components, temporal_bisection requires >=3 components.

errors.TRIAL_MIX_WRONG_PRIMITIVE: @trial_mix applies only to Mix/Mult combinators (free-operant); discrete-trial MTS/GoNoGo should use @testing instead
errors.TRIAL_MIX_MISSING_TYPE: type parameter is required; must be one of 'peak', 'reinstatement', 'temporal_bisection'
errors.TRIAL_MIX_MISSING_COMPONENTS: components parameter is required; must be a non-empty array<object>
errors.TRIAL_MIX_COMPONENT_MISSING_ROLE: Each component object must declare a 'role' string field
errors.TRIAL_MIX_COMPONENT_MISSING_REF: Each component object must declare a 'ref' integer field indexing the annotated Mix/Mult expression
errors.TRIAL_MIX_INVALID_REF: component 'ref' must index a valid component of the Mix/Mult expression (0 <= ref < len(components))
errors.TRIAL_MIX_DUPLICATE_REF: Distinct components within the same @trial_mix must reference distinct ref values
errors.TRIAL_MIX_UNKNOWN_ROLE: component 'role' value is not in the role catalog for the declared type
errors.TRIAL_MIX_MISSING_REQUIRED_ROLE: components array does not contain all required roles for the declared type (see required_role_sets_by_type)
errors.TRIAL_MIX_UNEXPECTED_ROLE: components array contains a role not allowed for the declared type
errors.TRIAL_MIX_COMPONENT_MISSING_ATTR: component object is missing a required attribute for its role (see role_catalog)
errors.TRIAL_MIX_COMPONENT_UNKNOWN_ATTR: component object contains an attribute not listed in the role catalog for its role
errors.TRIAL_MIX_INVALID_DURATION_LIST: probe.durations entries must be parseable as time_value strings (e.g., '3s', '150ms', '1min')
errors.TRIAL_MIX_PROBE_OUT_OF_ANCHOR_RANGE: For temporal_bisection, all probe.durations values must fall strictly between short_anchor.duration and long_anchor.duration

trial_mix.reference: Catania, A. C., & Reynolds, G. S. (1968). A quantitative analysis of the responding maintained by interval schedules of reinforcement. Journal of the Experimental Analysis of Behavior, 11(s3), 327-383. https://doi.org/10.1901/jeab.1968.11-s327; Roberts, S. (1981). Isolation of an internal clock. Journal of Experimental Psychology: Animal Behavior Processes, 7(3), 242-268. https://doi.org/10.1037/0097-7403.7.3.242; Eckard, M. L., & Kyonka, E. G. E. (2018). Differential reinforcement of response sequences in peak procedures. Behavioural Processes, 151, 111-118. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.beproc.2018.03.014; Baker, A. G., Steinwald, H., & Bouton, M. E. (1991). Contextual conditioning and reinstatement of extinguished instrumental responding. Quarterly Journal of Experimental Psychology, 43B, 199-218; Ostlund, S. B., & Balleine, B. W. (2007). Selective reinstatement of instrumental performance depends on the discriminative stimulus properties of the mediating outcome. Learning & Behavior, 35, 43-52. https://doi.org/10.3758/BF03196073; Church, R. M., & Deluty, M. Z. (1977). Bisection of temporal intervals. Journal of Experimental Psychology: Animal Behavior Processes, 3(3), 216-228. https://doi.org/10.1037/0097-7403.3.3.216; Fetterman, J. G., & Killeen, P. R. (1995). Categorical scaling of time: Implications for clock-counter models. Journal of Experimental Psychology: Animal Behavior Processes, 21(1), 43-63. https://doi.org/10.1037/0097-7403.21.1.43
